title: Warsh to Reiterate Fed’s Pledge to Get Inflation Down
summary: Kevin M. Warsh, since becoming chairman of the Federal Reserve, has declined to say whether he supports higher interest rates to tame price pressures.

title: Big Banks Smash Earnings Records, but ‘Tectonic’ Risks Loom
summary: The largest banks in the United States collectively raked in tens of billions of profits in the second quarter, despite the war in Iran and persistent inflation.

title: U.S. Workers Are More Productive Than Ever. A.I. Isn’t the Key.
summary: Companies have been getting more out of employees for several years. Tight labor markets, digitization and remote work are among the reasons.

title: Trump Says U.S. Will ‘Take Over’ Strait of Hormuz, and How Russia Made Japan a Den of Spies
summary: Plus, scientists find sugar in outer space.

title: Oil Rises Above $85 a Barrel as Gulf Clash Escalates
summary: Brent crude, the international benchmark, breached $85 per barrel for the first time in a month.

title: Trump’s Plan to Charge a Toll in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
summary: President Trump announced a 20 percent fee on cargo through the waterway, despite his own administration’s position that such fees violate international law.

title: In Rare Testimony, Supreme Court Justices Will Ask Congress for Security Funds
summary: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett, the first justices to appear before lawmakers since 2019, are requesting millions of dollars to address security concerns amid rising threats.

title: Red State, Blue Governor: It Could Happen in Iowa. Would It Matter?
summary: Democrats have real hope for candidates for governor in red states like Iowa and Ohio, but if Republicans seize supermajorities in the legislatures, their power would be limited.

title: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
summary: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.

title: Can Democrats Win Rural Voters Turning Away From Trump? It’s a Tough Sell.
summary: House candidates like Beth Macy, author of “Dopesick,” have crafted messages based on lifetimes in rural regions. But messages need an audience, and many minds are closed to the party.

title: At 91, He’s Hiking the Appalachian Trail. Again.
summary: Dale “Grey Beard” Sanders once set the record as the oldest person to complete the roughly 2,190-mile trail. Now, he’s back for his title.

title: Blanche Was a Driving Force in Retribution Campaign, Emails Show
summary: The cooperation of Todd Blanche in President Trump’s retribution against enemies will be a flashpoint in his confirmation hearing to be attorney general on Wednesday.

title: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
summary: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.

title: ICE Shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Through Open Passenger Window in Houston, Witnesses Say
summary: A congresswoman and a lawyer relayed witness accounts that diverge from Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s version of the fatal encounter.

title: Ukraine Wants to Make Its Own Antimissile System, Not Just Patriots
summary: The Ukrainian firm Fire Point has produced weapons that hit Russian territory. Its next goal, developing an antimissile system, is a much bigger challenge, experts said.

title: Europe, Flexing Muscle in Ukraine, Is Still on Sidelines in Iran
summary: In a show of collective strength, President Emmanuel Macron of France hosted European allies at the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris. Yet Europe is still a hostage to America’s foreign policy.

title: How a Promising Navy Drone Boat Program Got Mired in Politics
summary: Navy acquisition reforms upended a $2.1 billion contract competition. When it was over, several companies with ties to the Trump administration had made the cut.

title: The Strange Phenomenon of ‘Terminal Lucidity’
summary: As they near death, some dementia patients recover mental faculties assumed to be long lost. Researchers want to know why.

title: Agitation in Dementia Can Be Helped by Medical Cannabis, Study Suggests
summary: A combination of THC and CBD eased symptoms in an especially frail population: patients with advanced dementia near the end of their lives.

title: In Bangkok Bar Blaze, Signs of Fatal Lapses Repeated Across the World
summary: After a fire killed 30 people, the scrutiny has turned to whether the venue used flammable foam on its ceilings and had blocked exits.

title: Dark Sky Defenders Raise Alarm Along the Border of West Texas
summary: Residents fear that the Trump administration’s plans to build a border wall will threaten the region’s spectacular starry skies.

title: 75 Years Later, ‘The Catcher in the Rye’ Remains a Great American Novel
summary: Sure, “Catcher in the Rye” has its detractors. But the influence of its wised-up yet winningly innocent hero keeps it among the Great American Novels.

title: The Democratic Establishment Had This Coming
summary: The Democratic Party needs shaking up.

title: What Americans Need to Understand About China
summary: Kevin Rudd, a former prime minister of Australia, went back to school to try to understand how the Chinese president thinks.

title: Jon Stewart Mocks Mitch McConnell’s Recovery Photo
summary: The “Daily Show” host called the image “totally believable evidence that Mitch McConnell is not only alive, but the happiest boy in the hospital.”

title: In the Public Interest
summary: We look at the Trump administration’s attempts to pressure news organizations.

title: Pat Oliphant, Cartoonist Who Skewered the Powerful, Dies at 90
summary: The winner of countless awards, including a Pulitzer Prize, he was described in 1990 as “the most influential political cartoonist now working.”
